name: Barbados
tagline: Refined Caribbean Island Life with British Heritage
description: Barbados is the most polished island in the Caribbean, blending pristine beaches with British colonial heritage, excellent infrastructure, and a welcoming Welcome Stamp visa for remote workers and retirees. The cost of living is significantly higher than most Caribbean islands due to heavy import duties, but the quality of life, safety, and English-speaking culture make it a premium destination. Rum, cricket, and turquoise waters define the Bajan lifestyle.

quickFacts.visa: Welcome Stamp visa (12 months, renewable); standard tourist visa 6 months
quickFacts.safetyRating: High – one of the safest Caribbean islands
quickFacts.climate: Tropical maritime with consistent trade winds
quickFacts.currency: BBD (Barbados Dollar, pegged to USD at 2:1)

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Worthing or Hastings on the south coast; basic but near the beach

dining.description: Local Bajan food: fish cutters, macaroni pie at rum shops 2-3x/week; mostly cooking at home

healthcare.description: Basic private health insurance; Queen Elizabeth Hospital available for emergencies

transportation.description: Public minibuses (cheap and frequent) plus occasional taxi

entertainment.description: Beach life, Friday night fish fry at Oistins, cricket matches, hiking

1M.lifestyle: This is a tight budget for Barbados. Import duties push groceries to roughly double mainland US prices, utilities are expensive, and even basic private health insurance costs more than in Latin America. A one-bedroom apartment in Worthing or Hastings keeps you near the south coast beaches, but you cook at home most nights and take public minibuses. The beach is free and the Bajan culture is warm, but there is little financial cushion.

housing.description: Nice 2BR apartment in Christ Church or St. James with pool access

dining.description: Regular dining at Champers, Tides, and south coast restaurants; rum shop culture

healthcare.description: Comprehensive private insurance covering Bayview Hospital and Sandy Crest Medical

transportation.description: Small rental car or own vehicle; island is only 21 miles long

entertainment.description: Catamaran cruises, snorkeling, Crop Over festival, golf, beach clubs

2M.lifestyle: A nice two-bedroom in Christ Church or St. James with pool access gives you a comfortable base. You own a small car — the island is only 21 miles long, so everything is accessible — and dine at good restaurants like Champers several times a week. Comprehensive private health insurance and catamaran cruises or snorkeling trips are in budget. Costs are still higher than most Caribbean alternatives due to import duties on nearly everything.

housing.description: 3BR villa with pool on the west or south coast, or luxury beachfront condo

dining.description: Fine dining on the Platinum Coast at The Cliff, Lone Star; regular entertaining at home

healthcare.description: Premium international insurance with medical evacuation to Miami or Martinique

transportation.description: Own car, premium insurance; occasional island-hopping flights

entertainment.description: Sailing, premium golf at Sandy Lane or Apes Hill, spa days, Crop Over VIP

3M.lifestyle: A three-bedroom villa with a pool on the west or south coast, or a beachfront condo, provides a comfortable home. You dine at west coast restaurants like The Cliff and Lone Star for special occasions, own a car, carry premium international health insurance, and sail or golf regularly. The island's compact size means everything is within 30 minutes. Housing and groceries still take large shares of the budget due to Barbados's high import costs.

housing.description: Luxury beachfront villa on the west coast or estate home in Sandy Lane area

dining.description: Private chef plus fine dining at The Cliff, Cin Cin, and exclusive hotel restaurants

healthcare.description: Concierge medical with Miami specialist access; comprehensive international coverage

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle, inter-island private flights, occasional yacht charter

entertainment.description: Sandy Lane golf, yacht charters, polo matches, VIP Crop Over, Caribbean island hopping

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper, cook, gardener, and pool maintenance

luxuryExtras.description: Caribbean yacht cruises, first-class travel to UK and US, art collecting, philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: A beachfront villa on the west coast or an estate home near Sandy Lane provides premium island housing. A housekeeper, cook, and gardener manage the property, and you dine at the island's best restaurants without thinking about cost. Golf at Sandy Lane or Apes Hill, a nice car, and business class flights round out a comfortable life. Housing takes a large share even at this tier — Barbados's west coast real estate is among the most expensive in the Caribbean.

housing.description: Sandy Lane estate or sprawling beachfront compound on the platinum coast

dining.description: Personal chef team, exclusive catered events, private dining experiences

healthcare.description: Elite concierge medical, private physician, executive health program with Miami access

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, private yacht, chartered inter-island and transatlantic flights

entertainment.description: Private yacht, Sandy Lane membership, polo, art collecting, exclusive global events

domesticHelp.description: Full estate staff: chef, butler, housekeepers, groundskeepers, pool team, security, personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: Yacht ownership, first-class global travel, Barbados property portfolio, philanthropic foundation

10M.lifestyle: A Sandy Lane estate or sprawling beachfront compound on the platinum coast with full household staff — cook, housekeepers, groundskeepers — anchors daily life. You dine freely, fly first class internationally, own a boat, and have a nice car. Housing takes a very large share of this budget because Barbados's top-end real estate is priced at international luxury levels. The island's safety, English-speaking culture, and established infrastructure make it a polished but expensive Caribbean base.
